title: Market Brief — Monday, May 18, 2026
summary: GPU supply is bifurcated: A100s sit in oversupply at 85% availability, H100s are loose at 70%, and MI300X holds at 60%, but B200s are scarce at 25% and GB200s are in severe shortage at just 10% availability. The big price signal is H200 OCPI at $7.01/hr versus a $3.00 spot median, with 121.7% annualized volatility, meaning trade-settled prices are running 2.3x above listed spot as hyperscalers quietly hoard next-gen memory-bandwidth cards.
paragraphs: GPU supply is bifurcated: A100s sit in oversupply at 85% availability, H100s are loose at 70%, and MI300X holds at 60%, but B200s are scarce at 25% and GB200s are in severe shortage at just 10% availability. The big price signal is H200 OCPI at $7.01/hr versus a $3.00 spot median, with 121.7% annualized volatility, meaning trade-settled prices are running 2.3x above listed spot as hyperscalers quietly hoard next-gen memory-bandwidth cards.

On Llama 70B reference workloads, TCO-adjusted costs (1.25x spot) run $9.48-$11.90/M tokens on A100/H100/MI300X, meaning every provider offering medium-tier 70B inference is burning cash at losses of $6-$9 per million tokens. DeepSeek V3.2 at $0.45/M output and Meta Llama 4 Maverick at $0.60/M are priced so far below TCO that margin recovery is impossible without proprietary silicon. OpenAI grades Neutral on its premium o3 Pro margins but Burning Cash on gpt-oss-120b at $0.50/M output; Anthropic grades Strong on Opus 4.6 at $25/M output; Google grades Weak with Gemini 2.5 Pro at $10/M in a collapsing mid-tier; DeepSeek grades Burning Cash.

B200 inference at $4.02/M raw cost is the only GPU class generating positive margin at the high tier, making B200 capacity undervalued relative to the 25% scarcity signal and 109% OCPI volatility. Mid-tier open-source inference on A100/H100 is overvalued given negative margins exceeding -150%, so the trade is long B200 compute futures, short legacy A100 capacity.
bullets: 25% availability on B200s signals scarce compute with spot ranging $3.50-$5.50/hr.
10% availability on GB200s marks a severe shortage with no transparent spot pricing yet.
$7.01/hr H200 OCPI implies trade-settled GPU prices running 134% above the $3.00 spot median.
21.75 on the LLMflation Index means GPT-4 equivalent output now costs 78% less than at March 2023 launch.
$0.45/M output on DeepSeek V3.2 sits roughly $11/M below TCO-adjusted H100 production cost.
90.3% H100 utilization surged 10.5 percentage points in 30 days, the largest demand spike across all GPU classes.
